file_insights.changes: Added DefaultBodyLimit middleware layer to both production and test routers
file_insights.context: axum HTTP server implementation for POST /cards/new endpoint

patterns_discovered.detail: DefaultBodyLimit applied as a layer to Router, positioned after route definitions but before state binding
Identical middleware configuration applied to both test router and production router to ensure test validity
65536 literal used directly (64 KiB exactly) rather than symbolic constant or configuration

gotchas_discovered.detail: DefaultBodyLimit must be applied before .with_state() to ensure proper middleware stack initialization
Middleware must be added to test router setup, not just production code, or tests won't validate the constraint
65536 bytes is not parameterizable; if requirements change (e.g., for different endpoints or environments), modification is required

discoveries.approach_outcome: SUCCESS - minimal, focused change implementing request body size limit via idiomatic axum middleware. Clean integration with existing router setup. No refactoring or architectural changes needed.

recommendations.suggestion: Consider making the 65 KiB limit configurable via environment variable (BOP_MAX_BODY_BYTES) for deployment flexibility
Add integration test validating that requests exceeding 65 KiB are rejected with proper HTTP error status (413 Payload Too Large)
Document the 64 KiB limit in API specification and inline comments explaining the security rationale
If multiple endpoints emerge with different size requirements, consider per-route middleware configuration instead of global limit
